vibe_report.summary: A calm, warm Japandi interior tuned for a young family: quiet minimalism balanced with earthy textures, natural wood tones, and selective layering. Prioritize horizontal lines, low-profile furniture, tactile materials (linen, wool, rattan), and visible daylight from large openings. Add a compact standing-desk work corner in the living area and a small crib in the bedroom without disrupting the serene composition.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutrals anchored by light creamy off-white (#EDE7DF) and sand/latte midtones (#CBB698, #A88461), deep anchoring neutrals in matte charcoal/burnt umber (#6D6A5F, #2B2724). Use the lighter tones for major surfaces (walls, large textiles) and midtones for wood furniture; reserve the darkest tones for metal accents and planters to maintain readable contrast without high saturation.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient daylight with directional pulls from the living/dining glazing and bay windows. Maintain visible light-source logic: window and patio openings as primary sources, supplemented by dimmable rattan pendants and low-level lanterns for evening shots. Shadows should remain graphic but gentle — sculpted, not flat.
materials: wool and natural fibre rugs (tatami edge details)
textures: low-pile wool rug with visible weave
furniture_language: simple, tapered legs on wooden furniture
rounded table edges for dining nook
clean open shelving with curated objects
compact standing desk with cable-minimal setup
styling_rules: Keep surfaces decluttered; one sculptural object or ceramic vase per visible table.
Layer one textured rug under seating or dining area; avoid multiple competing patterns.
Use plants sparingly for organic color (one medium floor plant + one small tabletop).
Place the standing desk against a well-lit wall or beside the living glazing so it reads as a deliberate corner.
Crib must be simple, low profile, and aligned with room horizontals; dress with neutral linen and one soft throw.
avoid: high-gloss finishes or highly saturated colors
over-styling with many small decorative objects
floating furniture that breaks sightlines to windows/patio
introducing light sources that are not visible in the frame

room_guidance.headline: Calm family living with a cozy work corner and patio connection
room_guidance.guidance: Frame the living area to emphasize the sliding-glass connection to the patio and the low seating plane. Stage a compact standing desk as a dedicated work corner along a side wall (near natural light) so it reads functional yet unobtrusive. Use a single woven pendant over the coffee area, a low wooden coffee table, and a soft rug that visually links to the dining tatami/texture.
must_include: sliding glass door to patio visible
rattan pendant or low pendant fixture
must_avoid: desk clutter visible in wide frame

room_guidance.headline: Serene bedroom with family-safe crib and soft textiles
room_guidance.guidance: Keep the bed low and minimal; place the crib on the quieter side of the room with soft linens. Use layered textiles (wool rug, linen bedding) and one piece of calming wall art or wooden shelf. Maintain a clear circulation path to the closet and hall.
must_include: neutral bedding and one textured throw

room_guidance.headline: Compact kitchen that reads warm and functional
room_guidance.guidance: Use warm oak cabinets or open shelving, matte black hardware, and tidy countertops with one ceramic bowl or wooden cutting board. Keep sightline open to the dining nook; recover indirect daylight from dining bay to brighten the space. Avoid adding large island clutter.
must_include: warm wood cabinetry or wood-grain veneer
clean counters with one curated object
must_avoid: introducing new artificial light sources not visible in frame

room_guidance.headline: Intimate Japandi dining nook with rattan pendant
room_guidance.guidance: Center a round oak dining table under a woven rattan pendant. Use simple neutral chairs and a single stoneware vase as a centerpiece. Keep place settings minimal and highlight the bay-window light coming through the panels.
must_include: round oak dining table (scale to 7.8 x 8 ft bay)

room_guidance.headline: Outdoor covered patio as an extension of living
room_guidance.guidance: Stage as an outdoor living spot with a low bench, cushions, and a small side table. Allow visual continuity with the interior by matching wood tones and a single outdoor rug. Capture natural daylight and avoid staging that obscures the openings between patio and living/dining.
must_include: low wooden bench or small lounge seating
must_avoid: dense outdoor furniture sets that block openings

warnings: Dining nook is compact (7.83 x 8 ft). Use a small round table (max ~36-42" diameter) to avoid cramped framing.
Crib in the bedroom must not block egress path to hall or closet; place against side wall.
Standing desk in living should be compact and staged tidy to avoid reading as a home office; prefer wireless peripherals.
Run at least one edited image-pass for each main living shot to correct perspective and emphasize directional window pulls before video generation.

overall_mood: Calm, earthy, editorial Japandi — quiet horizons and tactile warmth
global_style_notes: AutoHDR workflow: run 1 image-pass (editorial interior correction) on each source photo; run a second pass only if more mood push is required.
Preserve architecture, sightlines, and measured furniture scale. Correct perspective but keep verticals true.
Derive all light from visible sources (windows/patio openings, pendants, bedside lamps). Do not invent hidden lights.
Keep motion stable and slow for first-pass tests (5s clips, 16:9, no audio).
negative_prompt: blur, distort, low quality, warped architecture, flicker, unstable camera, unrealistic lighting, muddy shadows, layout drift

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Day: soft directional daylight from sliding glass door to patio (primary). Supplement with warm rattan pendant over seating and soft fill from recessed lighting off-camera. Evening: dimmable pendant + low floor lamp for layered pools of warm light. Ensure shadows follow the door's direction and do not appear to come from ceiling fixtures that aren't visible.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Wide 24mm interior frame of the living area with sliding glass door to patio on one side. Low-profile linen sofa facing left, low oak coffee table centered on a low-texture wool rug, compact standing desk tucked to the right against side wall near window. Rattan pendant above coffee table. Materials: light oak, linen, woven rattan, neutral plaster walls. Color palette warm neutrals (#EDE7DF, #CBB698, #A88461). Keep verticals true, accurate human-scale proportions, and visible daylight coming through the glass door — no invented light. Lens: 24-28mm, eye-level camera ~3.5–5 ft, soft depth of field, sharp detail in foreground and midground.
room_plans.video_prompt: Super smooth slow dolly in, camera moves forward from the living entry toward the sliding-glass patio while keeping the low seating plane in frame; directional sunlight from the patio shifts across the sofa and coffee table, shadows move gently, consistent exposure, stable motion, cinematic, photorealistic.
must_avoid: overhead light sources not present in frame
furniture that blocks the patio opening

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Primary window provides medium directional daylight from the upper wall. Use bedside lamp as practical visible source for evening scenes. Keep bedroom exposures slightly warmer than living to convey intimacy; ensure crib shadowing is soft and consistent with window direction.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Bedroom interior with low platform bed centered along long wall, single exterior window on upper wall visible. Low-profile natural-wood crib placed on the quieter side of the room, neutral linen bedding, soft wool rug underfoot. Accessories: one bedside table with a dimmable lamp, small wall shelf with minimal objects. Materials: light oak, linen, plaster wall texture, unglazed ceramics. Maintain believable scale; do not place crib close to window or block access to closet. Lens: 35mm to 50mm for tighter, intimate framing; camera height ~3–4 ft.
room_plans.video_prompt: Static gentle zoom in, camera holds and slowly tightens on the crib corner and bedside while ambient daylight from the window sculpts soft shadows; exposure stable, realistic lamp practicals remain constant, cinematic, photorealistic.
must_avoid: introducing new light sources without visible fixtures

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Indirect daylight from the dining bay should provide soft fill. Use under-cabinet or pendant practicals only if visible in frame. Keep counters tidy; highlight warm wood tones and matte black hardware.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Compact kitchen view showing wood cabinetry, matte black hardware, clean counters with one wooden cutting board and ceramic bowl. Sightline opens to small dining bay on the right. Materials: warm oak veneer, matte black metal, neutral stone countertop. Keep architecture accurate and verticals straight. Lens: 28-35mm, mid-height camera ~4.5–5 ft to show counter plane and sightline into dining.
room_plans.video_prompt: Super smooth truck right to left, camera slides laterally to reveal the kitchen on the left and the dining nook beyond; indirect daylight from the dining bay softens across wood cabinetry, exposure held steady, stable motion, cinematic, photorealistic.
must_include: tidy counter with one curated object

room_plans.lighting_instruction: High natural light from the bay/window panels is primary. Rattan pendant should be a visible practical in frame (dimmable). Shadows should be soft but directional, moving consistently with the sun path in the video.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Small bay dining nook with round oak table (approx 36-42" diameter) centered under a woven rattan pendant. Two to four simple neutral chairs, single stoneware vase centerpiece. Bay/window with multiple vertical panels visible, letting in generous daylight. Materials: oak, rattan, linen. Keep scale true to 7.83 x 8 ft measured bay; avoid oversized furniture. Lens: 24-35mm, camera ~4 ft high to emphasize table plane and window geometry.
room_plans.video_prompt: Wide interior shot with a slow and smooth dolly in toward the round dining table; daylight from the bay windows grows slightly warmer as camera approaches, shadows crawl gently across the table, consistent exposure, stable motion, cinematic, photorealistic.

room_plans.lighting_instruction: High natural daylight; treat patio as an outdoor room with visible sky and natural plant light. Avoid adding interior-style artificial lighting unless practical lanterns are present and visible.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Covered patio with wide opening to the living/dining. Low wooden bench or small lounge chairs with neutral cushions, natural fibre outdoor rug, potted greenery. Materials: treated wood, woven textiles, terracotta or stone pots. Keep visual continuity with interior wood tones. Lens: 24mm for environmental coverage; camera positioned slightly outside opening to capture connection to interior.
room_plans.video_prompt: Very slow aerial drift upward and slightly inward, camera rises and drifts to reveal patio seating and the opening into the living area; natural daylight remains primary, exposure stable, shadows move subtly, cinematic, photorealistic.
must_include: low wooden bench or lounge seating
must_avoid: blocking the opening with staged objects

warnings: Follow AutoHDR flow: run an editorial image-pass (perspective correction and window pulls) for each main room image before video generation to avoid flat lighting and to keep verticals correct.
Respect measured room scale when staging (dining bay is small; choose table diameter <= 42"), and place crib so it does not obstruct egress or closet access.
Suppress any scene edits that introduce light sources not visible in the frame. Reject video outputs with bent walls, drifting furniture, or inconsistent shadow direction.
